title: barleria polhillii subsp. latiloba
intro: Barleria polhillii subsp. latiloba is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is also known as the "Nandi bushland barleria" and is native to eastern Africa. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The leaves of the plant can be prepared as a herbal tea to promote overall health and well-being, and have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues.
